content.claim: Hungary has the highest level of socioeconomic school segregation in the EU, with the most and least disadvantaged students showing the highest segregation rates in the 2022 PISA study
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Does not isolate causal contribution of 6/8-year gimnázium tracks specifically to overall segregation
Segregation driven by multiple independent factors (settlement geography, church-school autonomy, housing patterns)
Contested whether track reform alone would materially reduce segregation
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
